Review Dashboard Workflow

This HTML file is generated once after package generation, reviewer evidence, manifest entries, blocked previews, and package checks are current. It is derived from run files and package files; it is not a source of truth.
  1. Collect run inputsRead `run_manifest.yaml`, `review_notes.md`, `results.tsv`, generated or enriched package files, blocked briefs, and blocked design previews.
  2. Prepare mechanism storyboard promptspython3 scripts/generate_storyboard_prompts.py runs/valid_generate_and_curate
  3. Generate review-only storyboard imagesUse Codex image generation with the prompt files under `visual_storyboards/<activity_id>/`, then copy each final PNG to `mechanism_grid.png` and rerun the prompt script to refresh metadata. Prompt-only storyboard experiments are ignored when generated v1 images are present.
  4. Build runtime assets when requestedpython3 scripts/build_activity_assets.py runs/valid_generate_and_curate --mode generate_and_curateUse agent-generated illustrative PNGs and verified reference originals as inputs; write final runtime assets back into package-local `assets/` directories.
  5. Validate runtime asset outputspython3 scripts/validate_asset_build_outputs.py runs/valid_generate_and_curate
  6. Prepare prebuilt asset promptspython3 scripts/generate_asset_pilot_prompts.py runs/valid_generate_and_curate
  7. Generate prebuilt contact sheetsUse Codex image generation with `generated_assets_pilot/<activity_id>/<asset_id>/contact_sheet.prompt.md`, copy the selected PNG to `contact_sheet.png`, then rerun the prompt script so `asset.meta.yaml` and `asset_manifest.yaml` record status, timing, placement, fallback, and provenance.
  8. Integrate generated assetspython3 scripts/integrate_generated_assets.py runs/valid_generate_and_curate
  9. Validate integrated assetspython3 scripts/integrate_generated_assets.py --validate runs/valid_generate_and_curate
  10. Export integrated activity HTMLspython3 scripts/export_activity_html.py runs/valid_generate_and_curate
  11. Validate activity HTML exportspython3 scripts/export_activity_html.py --validate runs/valid_generate_and_curate
  12. Generate the dashboardpython3 scripts/generate_run_review.py runs/valid_generate_and_curate
  13. Validate the dashboardpython3 scripts/generate_run_review.py --validate runs/valid_generate_and_curate
  14. Record provenanceKeep `outputs.review_dashboard` pointing to `runs/valid_generate_and_curate/review.html` and record the generation and validation checks in the run manifest.

Review Criteria

Packages are still judged with the `program.md` Phase 3 10-dimension rubric. A package passes only when every applicable dimension passes; Dimension 8 may be N/A for non-mapping packages. Any FAIL or credible reviewer uncertainty requires repair before logging or checkoff.
#DimensionPass CriteriaWhy It Fails
1V1 Technical ComplianceNo runtime beat depends on blocked V1 capabilities such as OCR, pose/expression detection, IMU sensing, before/after comparison, or unsupported non-speech audio detection.Fail when the package promises capability the current runtime cannot safely execute or verify.
2Hook & TransitionStep 1 opens from emotional resonance, grows naturally from the trigger, and bridges into the activity without feeling like a sudden quiz.Fail when the opening is a cold task assignment, knowledge test, or disconnected transition.
3Edge Case CoverageEvery dialogue step covers ideal, unexpected, and no-response branches with validating redirects, wait timing, and concrete scaffolds.Fail when branches are missing, generic, or leave a child stuck after silence or an unexpected answer.
4IB CompletenessKey Concepts, Related Concepts, KUD, ATL skills, and closing dialogue are specific and match what the child actually does.Fail when IB language is vague, missing, or forced onto an activity that does not support it.
5Tier AppropriatenessLanguage, response length, number of rounds, task complexity, and vocabulary fit the declared target tier.Fail when a T0/T1/T2 package asks for too much, uses over-complex language, or under-delivers for the target age.
6Dialogue SpecificityAI lines are concrete spoken dialogue with tone markers and branch-specific reactions, not abstract narration.Fail when the runtime would need to invent missing prompts, feedback, or child-facing wording.
7Screen & UI CompletenessEach step names what appears, persists, changes, and how progress or payoff is represented on screen.Fail when screen states are generic, missing, or disconnected from the dialogue.
8Entity Mapping AlignmentMapping-informed designs use mapping concepts, themes, related concepts, tier language, anchor dimensions, and approved warm-start bridge logic. Non-mapping packages may be N/A.Fail when a mapping-informed package invents ungrounded facts or ignores required mapping anchors.
9Game FeelThe child experiences uncertainty, stakes, surprise, replay value, and an earned magic moment tied to repeated action.Fail when the activity is only a conversation or generic badge award without a game-like payoff.
10Mechanic Fidelity + Scaffold HonestyStep 3's repeated child action matches the declared mechanic and source promise, while the pillar/style scaffold supports rather than distorts it.Fail when the scaffold hides a mechanic mismatch, unsupported capability, or diluted source promise.

Generated Runtime Assets

2 of 2 assets built
Asset build mode `generate_and_curate` writes separate package-local runtime assets and source metadata. Contact sheets remain review-only artifacts.

Asset Smoke

asset_smoke

Generatedcat5unknowncollectunknownrequired_prebuiltunknown

Fixture package for asset build validation. ## Asset Brief | asset_id | asset_type | requiredness | generation_timing | use_step | display_location | purpose | prompt_en/source | display_behavior | fallback_behavior | |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---| | moss_icon | icon | required | pre_generated | prod.step_2 | center | Show moss. | Prompt. | Show the moss. | Voice fallback. | | orion_card | card | required | pre_generated | prod.step_3 | center | Show Orion. | Approved source. | Show the card. | Use source facts only. | ## Self-Evaluation Scorecard | # | Dimension | Score | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | 1 | D1 | PASS | ok | | 2 | D2 | PASS | ok | | 3 | D3 | PASS | ok | | 4 | D4 | PASS | ok | | 5 | D5 | PASS | ok | | 6 | D6 | PASS | ok | | 7 | D7 | PASS | ok | | 8 | D8 | PASS | ok | | 9 | D9 | PASS | ok | | 10 | D10 | PASS | ok |
